question: Which of the following correctly describes a 'hoax'?
choices: a truthful story backed by evidence
a trick intended to deceive people
a story published only in one newspaper

question: To 'verify sources' means a journalist ________.
choices: checks whether information comes from reliable places

question: Which of the following words means 'to say something officially'?
A story that is 'off the record' is information that ________.
choices: has gone viral on social media
is published in the public interest

question: The word 'dependable' means the same as ________.
The collocation 'shed light on' means to ________.
Which adjective describes a story that makes the reader feel deeply sad?
The phrase 'in the public interest' refers to information that ________.
choices: is very popular on social media
benefits or matters to the general public
is written by a famous journalist

question: The word 'bizarre' means ________.
To 'expose corruption' means to ________.
choices: report and reveal dishonest practices publicly
publish only one side of a story

question: The word 'pavement' contains which suffix that makes it a noun?
Which of the following words is formed with the suffix '-sion'?
The correct noun form of the adjective 'secure' is ________.
Choose the correctly derived word: 'The ________ crowd celebrated the team's victory.'
The word 'replacement' contains the suffix ________.
The tourists ________ (stand) at the bus stop for an hour before the bus arrived.
Nader had ink all round his mouth because he ________ (chew) his pen during the exam.
Abbas ________ (work) at the East Hotel for exactly 25 years on the day he disappeared.
While everyone ________ (leave) the dining room, the rumours began.
Shelley's family were able to ________ until the storm passed.
Barely ________ the researchers arrived at the site when a sandstorm began.
Little ________ the editor know that the story was a complete fabrication.
Seldom ________ such a convincing piece of fake news been published.
The rewritten form of 'They had only just left when the house exploded' using 'Scarcely' is ________.
choices: Scarcely they had left when the house exploded.
Scarcely had they left when the house exploded.
Scarcely the house had exploded when they left.
Scarcely left had they when the house exploded.

question: Under no circumstances ________ students allowed to bring their phones into the examination hall.
The narrator in 'The Man in Black' realised she was wrong about the man because ________.
choices: the police confirmed he was innocent
she saw him save the old woman from a falling crane
the old woman told her the man was her relative
the man apologised for frightening everyone

question: During the cyclone, the villagers took shelter ________.
The word 'composed' when describing a photograph means ________.
The comment adverb 'Theoretically' in a sentence suggests something ________.
choices: is possible in principle but may not be true in practice
happened in the very distant past

question: In the sentence 'He yelled loudly in my ear', the adverb 'loudly' is placed ________.
choices: at the beginning of the clause
at the end of the clause

question: The second hotel article was concerned that the new roads would ________.
choices: bring more tourists than the area could handle
affect bird and animal life in a sensitive nature area
block access to the local school
destroy historic buildings in the area

question: The sentence 'By the time he died, he had written a lot of letters' uses the Past Perfect Simple to show ________.
choices: an ongoing action at the time of death
an action in progress before a certain point
an experience completed before a specific past time
a repeated action over a period of time

question: The ancient 'kites' found in the Black Desert of Jordan were ________.
choices: objects flown in the sky by ancient people
large birds that ancient people hunted
long walls used to trap animals for food

question: Archaeologists thought the kite walls at Jibal al-Khashabiyeh might be ________.
choices: possibly the oldest kite walls in the world
the remains of an ancient city
the first evidence of writing in the region

question: The debate phrase 'There have been several cases where this has happened...' is used to ________.
choices: provide examples to back up an argument
refer to statistics from a study

question: Issa's advice for debating says a debater should ________.
choices: be passionate but not aggressive or overly emotional
use extreme language to convince the audience
only argue for positions they personally believe

question: The phrase 'I'll never do that again.' is used at the ________ of an anecdote.
The comment adverb 'Unbelievably' at the start of a sentence shows the speaker thinks what they are describing is ________.
A 'gossip column' in a newspaper is an article about ________.
A text type that tells the personal story of a famous person is called ________.
